meta.title: Iceland Ring Road — South & Golden Circle
meta.subtitle: 5-day campervan loop · Jul 1–5, 2026
meta.timezoneNote: Iceland = NYC + 4 hrs (Iceland stays on GMT year-round). Early-July daylight ~3 AM–midnight, never fully dark.
meta.clockNote: All times are 24-hour, Iceland local (GMT). Each stop stores an explicit `arrive` plus an editable `duration` (minutes). `arrive` is computed from the day's `dayStart` + preceding durations + drive legs; the app recomputes it live from real Google Directions drive times and can save changes back here. `driveToNext` is null in this file — the app fills it from the live Directions leg.

flights.outbound: Delta DL246 — JFK 11:50 PM Tue Jun 30 → KEF 9:30 AM Wed Jul 1
flights.return: Delta DL247 — KEF 11:25 AM Sun Jul 5 → JFK 1:27 PM

budget.note: Rough per-person on-the-ground cost (excludes flights + camper rental). Iceland removed fuel taxes Jan 2026, so diesel dropped to ~260 ISK/L. Skip the Camping Card — not worth it under ~10 nights.

perPerson.skyLagoon: $75–95 (Sér pass — full 7-step Skjól Ritual; book ahead)
perPerson.parkingFees: ~$30 (7 paid lots × ~$7, split across the car)

mustBookAhead: Sky Lagoon Sér pass / 7-step ritual (book a week+ ahead for July)
Jökulsárlón boat tour if you want it (a few days)

logistics: Day 1: after a 9:30 AM landing you won't be on the road until ~11:30 AM (peak-summer customs + camper-depot shuttle). No firm plans before midday.

Sky Lagoon is on Day 1 (arrival) — ~40 min NE of the depot toward Reykjavík (an out-and-back spur, opposite the South Coast), chosen for its 7-step Skjól Ritual + ocean infinity edge. Book a midday SÉR slot (~12:30) as a buffer against customs/pickup delays.

LATE-EVENING RHYTHM (Days 1–3): early-July daylight runs ~03:10 sunrise to ~23:50 sunset and never gets dark, so Days 1–3 deliberately run into the long evening — the last real activity ends ~22:00 in the low midnight-sun light, then a short drive/wind-down, bedtime ~23:30, and a sleep-in to ~08:30 wake (no alarms). Day 1 still starts 09:30 (fixed 09:30 landing); Days 2–3 start ~09:30/10:30 off an ~08:30 wake plus morning routine + drive to the first stop.

SLEEP RULE: 9 hours of sleep every night, preserved on the late shift. Each day's `overnight` has an explicit bedtime/wake/sleepHours block, and the next day's dayStart leaves room for 9h sleep + morning routine + drive to the first stop. Days 1–3: bed ~23:30 → wake ~08:30 (9h). The app warns (amber/red) if editing durations pushes bedtime so late that 9h before the required wake becomes impossible.

TRADEOFF — last night (Jul 4→5) stays EARLY: the 07:30 camper drop (at the Fitjabraut depot, ~5 min from KEF) forces a ~06:00 wake, so to still get 9h you go to bed ~21:00. Day 4 (Golden Circle) is therefore the exception to the late rhythm — it starts early (09:00, off a 07:30 wake on Night 3) and winds down ~19:00 at Reykjadalur (with dinner in Hveragerði) so you're at the Keflavík base by ~20:30 and in bed by 21:00. Night 3's overnight (bed 22:30 / wake 07:30) is the bridge: it's earlier than Days 1–2 precisely so Day 4 can start early. This is the one night 9h forces an early bedtime.

Day 5: wake ~06:00, fill the tank at the N1 by the depot, drop the camper at the 7:30 AM slot (Fitjabraut depot, NOT the terminal — ~5 min away; drop-off takes 30–45 min). Aim to be at KEF by ~08:35, ~2h50m before the 11:25 flight — July is a KEF peak month and US flights are time-sensitive, so this buffer matters. If the depot shuttle is off-cycle, take a taxi. ⚠️ Verify with the rental: depot opens by 7:30, and the drop DATE is Jul 5 (your booking showed Jul 6 — confirm).

days.title: Arrival, Sky Lagoon & Waterfalls

overnight.note: Night 1 base — large, clean campground in Vík. Late-evening rhythm: in after Skógafoss, wind down, bed ~23:30 → wake 08:30 (9h). Reynisfjara is 10 min away, so a 09:00 Day-2 start is relaxed.

days.narr: Land 9:30 AM, clear customs, ride to the depot and pick up the camper (~11:30 AM). Soak off the red-eye at Sky Lagoon — the full 7-step Skjól Ritual with an infinity edge over the Atlantic — then point the camper east on Route 1, grabbing a late lunch + resupply in Selfoss before two of the South Coast's biggest waterfalls and the black-sand coast at Vík. Easy first day — you'll be jet-lagged, and the long evening light means no rush. (Sky Lagoon is a NE spur toward Reykjavík, opposite the South Coast, so expect a bit more arrival-day driving.)

stops.note: Land DL246. Clear passport/customs (allow 30–45 min, peak morning). Before leaving the terminal: buy duty-free alcohol (cheapest in Iceland) at the arrivals Vínbúðin and grab a tourist SIM/eSIM. Then head out front to grab a ride to the camper depot.

🚐 PICK UP THE CAMPER — booking ref PAUT2577, Reykjavik Depot, Fitjabraut 3, 260 Keflavík (~5 min from terminal). NOTE: no complimentary airport shuttle from this depot — take a taxi/rideshare from the terminal front. At the depot: paperwork, insurance review, vehicle walkthrough + damage inspection (📸 photograph all existing dents before signing). Peak-July pickups can run slow (1–2 hr waits reported) — budget ~75 min and don't book a tight Sky Lagoon slot. Aim wheels-on-road ~11:45.

Route 41 (Reykjanesbraut) northeast toward the capital area, then south into Kópavogur. ~42 km, ~40 min. NOTE: Sky Lagoon sits NE of KEF toward Reykjavík — the opposite direction from the South Coast — so this is an out-and-back spur. Worth it for the post-red-eye soak.

stops.bookNote: Book the SÉR pass — it includes the full 7-step Skjól Ritual + a private changing suite. Timed entry, reserve ahead for July (less prone to selling out than Blue Lagoon, but lock a midday slot). The 7 steps: 1) warm lagoon soak → 2) cold plunge → 3) sauna (ocean-view) → 4) cold mist → 5) sea-salt body scrub → 6) steam room → 7) shower. Allow ~2–2.5 hrs to do the ritual unhurried.
stops.parking: Free on-site lot, short walk to reception.
stops.note: Post-red-eye reset with an INFINITY edge over the Atlantic — the signature shot. Do the full 7-step Skjól Ritual to shake off the flight. Book ~12:30 entry for buffer against customs/pickup delays. Grab a bite at the on-site Smakk Bar (Icelandic small plates) or eat in Selfoss on the drive east. Then it's a ~2 hr push to the South Coast.

South out of Kópavogur to Route 1 / Ring Road, then east past Selfoss & Hvolsvöllur. ~120 km, ~2 hrs. Stop in SELFOSS (~45 min in) for a late lunch + fuel + grocery resupply — last big town before the South Coast. (Tryggvaskáli or Kaffi Krús are solid lunch spots.)

stops.parking: Paid lot right at the falls, ~800–1,000 ISK flat (pay by plate / Parka app); ~5–6 min flat paved walk to the falls. (Free lot off Rte 1 ~15 min walk away if you'd rather not pay.)
stops.note: The one you can walk BEHIND. You'll get soaked — full rain gear. Pop over to Gljúfrabúi (hidden falls, ~5 min walk) too. Evening light is great and crowds thin — no rush in the midnight sun.
Route 1 / Ring Road east. ~30 km, ~25 min.

stops.parking: Lot at the base, ~1,000 ISK flat / 8 hrs (pay via Parka app); ~2 min walk to the foot of the falls, ~7 min up the staircase to the top viewpoint.
stops.note: Massive and thunderous, rainbows on sunny days. Climb the stairs to the top viewpoint — you've got light until ~midnight, so linger into the low evening light (~10:30 PM finish).
Route 1 / Ring Road east. ~34 km, ~30 min.

BIG SHOP for the whole east leg — cook at the Vík and Svínafell campsite kitchens. Krónan is the supermarket in Vík (NO Bónus here), on the main road by the N1. ⚠️ Krónan closes ~20:00 — if you're rolling in late tonight it'll be SHUT, so plan to shop EARLIER (it's also your Day-2 breakfast stop) or stock up before. Top off fuel at the N1/Orkan next door. 🍔 DINNER (the trip's tightest food moment): Smiðjan Brugghús or Suður-Vík — BUT both kitchens close ~21:00. Tonight, just cook at the campsite.
Local roads through Vík village. ~3 km, ~5 min.

stops.bookNote: First-come, large site (250 capacity) — rarely fills, just arrive.
stops.note: Night 1 base. Large, clean, cozy common kitchen. Showers ~300kr (coins at reception). Crowded in summer. Wind down, bed shortly after arrival. Reynisfjara is 10 min away — hit it first thing tomorrow.

days.title: Black Sand, Canyons, Glaciers & Icebergs

overnight.note: Night 2 base — overnight east at Svínafell to avoid backtracking. Adding Dyrhólaey (puffins) makes this the latest night: bed ~23:50 → wake 08:50 to keep the full 9h (Day 3 is a relaxed light-driving day, so a slightly later wake is fine). If you'd rather not run this late, trim the puffin stop or shave time at Svartifoss/Diamond Beach.

days.narr: Start at the black beach right by Vík, then the most dramatic stretch of the trip: a slot canyon, the Skaftafell glacier area, and the famous iceberg lagoon + Diamond Beach. Overnight east at Svínafell to avoid backtracking — the move that makes this day work.

stops.note: Wake ~08:30 (9h sleep from a ~23:30 bed). Coffee, shower, pack up the camper, breakfast prep. ~1 hr before rolling out to the first stop. Camped at Vík Camping.
🥐 Lava Bakery at the Krónan complex in Vík opens 09:00 — fresh bread, pastries, coffee. Or cook at camp. Pop into Krónan next door for trail snacks/water — last big supermarket before the east.
Route 1 then Route 215. ~8 km, ~10 min.

stops.parking: Paid lot at the beach — 1,000 ISK (~$7) for 3 hrs via Parka app (upper lot 750 ISK). ~2 min walk to the sand.
stops.note: Moved here from Day 1 — natural first stop from Vík. Basalt columns + sea stacks. DANGER: sneaker waves kill people here. Never turn your back on the ocean.
Back to Route 1, then Route 218. ~12 km, ~15 min.

stops.parking: Free. Lower lot always open; Upper lot (by lighthouse, ~200 m walk) reached via steep narrow gravel road — fine in dry summer, 4x4 safer. During nesting season the upper promontory is foot-access only 09:00–19:00, so a daytime visit is fine; check the gate.
stops.note: 🐦 PUFFIN spot — peak nesting season is exactly now (June–early Aug). Dramatic promontory with a lighthouse + rock arch, basically no detour. Lower viewpoint has puffins; bring a zoom lens. Free, ~30–40 min.
Route 218 → Route 1 east past Vík, then Route 206. ~75 km, ~55 min.

stops.parking: Lower lot 1,000 ISK (~$7) via Parka app — license-plate cameras, fines if unpaid. Upper lot on F206 near the main viewpoint is free. ~5 min walk to the rim trailhead.
stops.note: Serpentine slot canyon (the 'Justin Bieber canyon'). ~1 hr+ round trip on the rim trail.
Route 206 → Route 1 east into Kirkjubæjarklaustur. ~12 km, ~10 min.
⛽ THE most important fill of the trip. LAST reliable fuel before the long empty stretch past Skaftafell to Jökulsárlón — nothing until Höfn, well east of the lagoon. Top up even if half-full.

🍽️ Systrakaffi, Klausturvegur 13, open daily 12:00–21:00 — Arctic char/pizza/cakes, no reservations, ideal drop-in. Or skip and hold out for the Glacier Goodies food truck at Skaftafell (langoustine soup / fish & chips, ~11:30–20:00).
Route 1 east across the Skeiðarársandur outwash plain. ~70 km, ~45 min.

stops.parking: Skaftafell Visitor Centre lot — 1,040 ISK (~$8) via Parka, valid till midnight (in/out OK). Gives a 50% reciprocal discount on Jökulsárlón parking the same day, so pay here. Glacier-view path + Svartifoss trailhead start at this lot.
stops.note: Part of Vatnajökull National Park. Quick glacier-view stop + trailhead for Svartifoss. (Do NOT book a guided glacier walk today — that's 6–8 hrs and would kill the lagoon.)

stops.parking: Same Skaftafell Visitor Centre lot (already paid) — no separate fee. Trail starts here: ~1.5 km each way, steady uphill, ~45 min up / ~35 min down on foot.
stops.note: The 'black waterfall' over hexagonal basalt columns. ~2 hrs round trip incl. time at the falls. Pair with the glacier viewpoint.
Route 1 east. ~57 km, ~45 min.

🦞 NOTE TIMING: the Heimahumar 'Local Langoustine' truck right at the lagoon (langoustine roll + acclaimed soup, genuinely local) closes before 7pm — if you arrive after, it's likely shut. Plan B: Frost @ Fjallsárlón (~15 min west, sit-down) or cook at Svínafell tonight. Skip the 1h40m detour to Höfn for langoustine (mostly imported now). If you can shave time earlier, push to reach the truck by ~18:30.

stops.bookNote: Lagoon viewing is free; parking is the only fixed cost. OPTIONAL amphibian boat tour ~7,100 ISK/$51 pp (every ~30 min, popular slots fill — book a few days ahead); Zodiac ~$120 pp books out fast. Boat not required.
stops.parking: Main lagoon lot — 1,040 ISK (~$8) via Parka, BUT half-price if you paid at Skaftafell today (reciprocal). One payment also covers both Diamond Beach lots until midnight. Free overflow lot ~2.3 km away if main is full.
stops.note: Icebergs calving off the glacier drift across the lagoon. Watch for seals. A trip highlight — give it real time in the long evening light.
Route 1 just across the bridge/road from the lagoon. ~1.5 km, ~3–5 min. Parking already covered by lagoon payment.

stops.parking: Both Diamond Beach lots (east + west of the river) are covered by your Jökulsárlón payment — no separate fee. ~2–3 min walk to the ice on black sand.
stops.note: Ice chunks wash up like diamonds on black sand. Go at LOW TIDE for the classic look. Across the road from the lagoon. Evening light is gorgeous here — finishes in the golden low-sun glow.
Route 1 west back past Skaftafell. ~60 km, ~50 min.
Night 2 base — repeatedly called the best site on the South Coast. ~$12pp. Great kitchen, clean showers. Bed shortly after arrival; the long evening at Diamond Beach is worth the late arrival.

days.title: Transit West + Hot Spring

overnight.note: Night 3 base — camp in Flúðir right at the Secret Lagoon. This night PRECEDES the early Day 4, so it stays earlier: a long evening soak ends ~21:15, bed 22:30 → wake 07:30 (9h); Brúarfoss is ~25 min away for an early Day-4 start.

days.narr: Your longest drive, heading back west to set up the Golden Circle. Light on stops by design. Cook breakfast at camp, then a big westward transit — fuel up in Vík, long gaps between stations. A wild-pool soak at Seljavallalaug on the way, resupply + lunch in Selfoss, Kerið Crater, then a long, unhurried evening soak at the Secret Lagoon.

stops.note: Wake ~08:50 (9h sleep from a ~23:50 bed). Coffee, shower, pack up the camper, breakfast prep. ~1 hr before rolling out to the first stop. Camped at Svínafell Campground (near Hof).

BREAKFAST: cook at camp before the long haul west — thin food options out here. Skaftafell Bistro (at the visitor centre, ~2 min away) is the only nearby café if you'd rather grab a coffee/pastry to go. Pack up; today is mostly driving.
Route 1 west ~135 km, ~1h45. ⛽ FUEL STOP in Vík — top off at the Orkan (cheaper, 24/7) or N1; it's a long stretch west with thin stations. Quick break only.
Route 1 west then Route 242 (Raufarfell turnoff) ~42 km, ~35 min. Road ends at a small free gravel parking lot.

stops.parking: Free gravel lot at the end of Route 242. ~15–20 min walk in each way along a rocky riverbed/stream-crossing trail — wear good shoes. No facilities at the lot.
stops.note: Historic pool hidden in a valley. Lukewarm (not hot), rough/basic changing rooms. Trimmed to ~75 min (incl. the 15–20 min walk each way) so the day lands on a 22:30 bed — it's a quick wild dip rather than a long lounge, which fits since you've got the long Secret Lagoon soak this evening. Skip it entirely if you'd rather sleep in.
Back out Route 242 to Route 1 west ~95 km, ~1h15. Selfoss is the big services hub (N1) — refuel here too if needed.
LUNCH: Kaffi Krús (burgers/pizza/cakes, good value, ~11–22) or Tryggvaskáli (Iceland's oldest restaurant building, more refined, from 11:30). Grab-and-go: GK Bakarí.
Resupply for the Golden Circle back half — Bónus is Iceland's cheapest chain (open 10–20). Stock up to cook at the Flúðir campsite. Parking: free lot at the store.
North on Route 35 ~13 km, ~15 min.

stops.parking: Free gravel lot right at the crater, beside the entrance — no walk-in. Toilets on site (close 9pm).
stops.note: Volcanic crater lake with a red rim — small entry fee paid at the lot. Walk the rim + down to the water at an easy pace.
Route 35 north then east toward Flúðir ~39 km, ~30 min.

stops.bookNote: Lagoon: 4,500 ISK/$33 pp, book a day or two ahead in summer (towel/suit rental ~$8). Flúðir campsite right here ~$16 pp + tax, no booking needed.
stops.parking: Free lagoon parking lot right at the entrance; the Flúðir campsite shares the same site, so you park once and stay. No walk-in.
stops.note: Iceland's oldest pool — less touristy than Blue/Sky. Reservation recommended. With the short driving day, settle in for a long ~3+ hr evening soak in the midnight-sun light, ending ~21:15.

DINNER: Minilik, an affordable Ethiopian spot right here in Flúðir (big portions, great for veg; Tue–Sun 18:00–21:00) — note it closes 21:00, so eat BEFORE the soak if you arrive past close, or cook at the Flúðir campsite tonight with the Bónus haul. You're parked here either way.
Night 3 — camp right at the Secret Lagoon. Bed ~22:30 (earlier, because Day 4 starts early) → wake 07:30.

days.title: The Golden Circle

overnight.place: Happy Campers Keflavík (for the early Day-5 drop)
overnight.note: Night 4 base — Happy Campers Keflavík campsite (~$14 pp), closest to the depot for the morning drop. TIGHT NIGHT: the 07:30 depot drop means wake ~06:00 (pack up + ~10 min drive to Fitjabraut by 07:30), so bed by 21:00 for 9h. Be at this base by ~20:30 — Day 4 winds down earlier than Days 1–3.

days.narr: The classic trio plus a blue-water bonus, rebuilt as an explicit chain. Brúarfoss turquoise warm-up, then the Gullfoss–Strokkur core, a greenhouse lunch, Þingvellir's rift valley, and a Reykjadalur hot-river soak with pizza in Hveragerði before the push west. Everything front-loaded so you reach the Keflavík base by ~20:30 for the tight pre-drop night.

stops.note: Wake ~07:30 (9h sleep from a ~22:30 bed). Coffee, shower, pack up the camper, breakfast prep. ~1 hr before rolling out to the first stop. Camped at Flúðir (near Secret Lagoon).

stops.nm: Breakfast — Efstidalur II (or cook at camp)
stops.note: Flúðir/Reykholt has thin early options. Efstidalur II farm café is en route on Rte 37 but opens ~10:00 — too late for a 08:30 start. Best move: cook breakfast at camp before leaving, or grab a quick bite at the Flúðir N1/Samkaup. Treat this as a real fuel-up step before the drive cluster.
~25 min, ~20 km via Rte 30/35 then local road to the parking area.

stops.parking: Use the NEW official parking lot (built 2023), not the old far trailhead. The new lot is only ~500 m / 5-min walk from the falls — 30–45 min round trip total. The old trailhead is a ~7 km / 1-hr-each-way hike; skip it entirely.
stops.note: Unreal turquoise-blue water. Pay ~750 ISK at the new lot, short walk in, soak it in, back out.
~25 min, ~25 km via local road to Rte 37 then Rte 35 north.

stops.parking: Large free paved lot right at the visitor centre / café. Upper viewing platform is a flat 2-min walk; lower platform is a short stair/path descent (~5 min down). No fee.
stops.note: The 'Golden Falls' — a thundering two-tier monster. Do both upper and lower platforms. Café on site if you want a coffee (lunch is booked at Friðheimar later).
~12 min, ~10 km via Rte 35 south.

stops.parking: Pay-parking lot across Rte 35 from the geothermal field, beside the Geysir Center/shop. Flat ~2-min walk to Strokkur. Pay via plate-recognition app or kiosk.
stops.note: Strokkur erupts every 5–10 min, up to 30 m — wait for 2–3 blasts. Optional uphill path for the wider geothermal-field + valley view. Then drive to your booked lunch.
~15 min, ~14 km south on Rte 35 toward Reykholt.

stops.bookNote: BOOKING REQUIRED — reserve weeks ahead; sells out in July. Fallbacks if unbooked: Friðheimar's own Winebar/Bistro, Gullfoss Kaffi (refillable lamb soup), or Efstidalur II (burgers + cowshed ice cream).
stops.parking: Free gravel lot at the farm entrance, steps from the greenhouse door.
stops.note: Eat among the tomato vines; refillable soup-and-bread buffet, kitchen ~11:30–16:00. The one meal worth engineering the day around.
~40 min, ~45 km via Rte 35 → 37 → 365 → 36 to the Hakið (P1) area.

stops.cost: ~$7 (1000 ISK) parking (covers all lots all day)
stops.parking: Park at P1 Hakið (visitor centre / Almannagjá viewpoint) — best single base: direct access down the Almannagjá rift gorge, the boardwalk, Law Rock, and the church. Walk the gorge ~15 min north to Öxarárfoss, OR move the car to P2 Öxarárfoss (closest lot to the falls) since the same daypass covers all lots. P5 (Valhöll) is for Silfra — skip unless diving.
stops.note: Walk between the North American & Eurasian plates; original parliament site + Öxarárfoss waterfall. Biggest time block (~2 hrs). Do Almannagjá from P1, then Öxarárfoss via the gorge path or a quick car-hop to P2.
~50 min, ~55 km via Rte 36 → 35 → Rte 1 south to the Hveragerði trailhead.

stops.parking: Pay-parking at the Hveragerði trailhead lot (north edge of town, end of the road past the campsite). Pay ~700 ISK via the Parka app or kiosk. From the lot it's a ~3 km / ~45-min uphill hike each way to the soakable stretch of river — wear proper shoes, bring a towel; boardwalk + changing screens at the river.
stops.note: Naturally hot river up a ~3 km uphill hike. ⚠️ TRIMMED TO ~75 min for the tight pre-flight night — that's only a QUICK taste (the full hike is ~45 min uphill EACH WAY). Realistic options in 75 min: hike partway up the geothermal valley and turn back, OR do a fast up-soak-down if you're quick hikers and skip lingering. If you want the full hot-river experience, you'd need ~2.5–3 hrs — but that blows the 21:00 bed before the 06:00 wake. Given you've already soaked at Sky Lagoon, Secret Lagoon & Seljavallalaug, a quick look here is a fair trade. Pay ~700 ISK parking via Parka.
~6 min, ~3 km back down into Hveragerði town centre.

stops.parking: Street / small lot in Hveragerði centre, right by the restaurant.
stops.note: Wood-fired pizza + geothermal in-house brews; kitchen closes ~21:00 so don't arrive after ~20:30. Eat here BEFORE the push west — no good late dinner options en route. Alt closer to base: Kaffi Duus in Keflavík (harbourside seafood, kitchen to ~22:00) if you'd rather eat after the drive.
~1 h 5 min, ~85 km via Rte 1 west past Reykjavík onto Rte 41 (Reykjanesbraut) to Keflavík.

stops.parking: Campsite parking on arrival; ~10 min from the Fitjabraut depot for the 07:30 drop.
stops.note: Aim to arrive ~20:30–21:00. Bed ~21:00, wake 06:00 — pack up + ~10-min drive to the camper depot for the 07:30 drop before the 11:25 flight. Tightest night of the trip; that's why Day 4 wound down early.

days.title: Departure
days.narr: Clean exit, no slack. Wake ~06:00 at Happy Campers Keflavík and pack up while it's quiet. Fill the tank at the N1 self-service pumps next to the depot (Fitjabraut 2) BEFORE drop-off — the refuel surcharge is punishing. Either grab early breakfast at Hotel Keflavík's KEF Café (opens 05:00, hot food from 06:00) or skip it and eat airside after security. Drop the camper at the 07:30 slot (Fitjabraut 3 — NOT the terminal, ~3 km away), allow 30–45 min for inspection, then shuttle/taxi 5 min to KEF for ~08:35 — about 2h50m before the 11:25 Delta flight (DL247). ⚠️ Before today: confirm the drop-off DATE is Jul 5 (booking form showed Jul 6) and that the depot opens by 07:30.

stops.note: Up ~06:00. Strip bedding, repack, empty the fridge/bins, and do a final sweep of the van (overhead, door pockets, under seats) — anything left behind is gone. Settle campsite check-out and dump grey water/toilet if the camper has them. Roll out by ~06:45.
~3–4 km, ~6–8 min from the campsite to the N1 self-service station at Fitjabraut 2, right beside the rental depot.

stops.nm: Fuel Up — N1 Fitjabraut 2 (fill BEFORE drop-off)
stops.note: ⛽ FILL THE TANK TO FULL here — the rental's refuel surcharge is brutal vs. pump price. N1 automated self-service (sjálfsafgreiðslustöð), unmanned 24/7, open well before 07:30. Pay at the pump with a chip-and-PIN card (have a 4-digit PIN ready — Icelandic pumps reject signature-only US cards). 📸 Keep the fuel receipt to prove a full return. ~200 m from the depot.

Only real hot breakfast open this early: KEF Café at Hotel Keflavík (~3–4 km / 5–7 min into town). Opens 05:00; hot food from 06:00. ☕ DECISION: only detour here if you left the campsite early and are comfortably ahead — it adds ~15–20 min round-trip and pulls you AWAY from the depot. If tight, SKIP IT and eat airside after security. Don't let breakfast threaten the 07:30 drop or the 11:25 flight.

Drop at the Fitjabraut 3 depot (NOT the terminal — ~3 km / 5 min away). Budget 30–45 min: staff walk-around inspection, fuel-gauge check (show the N1 receipt), mileage read, paperwork. 📸 BEFORE handing over keys, photograph EVERY panel, windscreen, all four tyres/rims and the undercarriage in daylight — gravel chips are the #1 dispute. Hand back all keys, fobs, gas bottles, accessories. TRANSFER: the free depot shuttle is HOURLY + pre-booked — if inspection finishes off-cycle, don't wait, take a taxi (~5 min, ~3,000–4,000 ISK). ⚠️ VERIFY with the rental (+354 539 6479): (a) depot open by 07:30, (b) drop DATE is Jul 5 not Jul 6 (your booking form showed Jul 6).
~3 km, ~5 min from Fitjabraut 3 to the KEF departures terminal via the pre-booked depot shuttle or backup taxi.

At the terminal ~08:35, ~2h50m before the 11:25 AM Delta flight (DL247) to the US. July is peak season at KEF and US-bound flights are time-sensitive — Isavia advises 2.5–3 hrs early. 💳 VAT/TAX-FREE: if claiming a refund, hit the tax-free desk in departures FIRST (long summer queues; some goods inspected before check-in). Then bag-drop, security (06:00–09:00 is the rush, allow 30–50 min), passport control, boarding. Eat airside if you skipped breakfast. Fly home.
